findings.reason: ECC signatures and ECDH key agreement are quantum-vulnerable public-key cryptography.
findings.recommendation: Identify protocol owners and track standards-approved PQC or hybrid replacements for signing and key agreement.

findings.reason: SSH host and user keys are authentication assets that may need algorithm migration.
findings.recommendation: Inventory host/user key locations, rotate away from legacy algorithms where possible, and track PQC SSH support.

findings.reason: TLS certificates and RSA-authenticated ciphersuites are priority assets for PQC transition planning.
findings.recommendation: Inventory certificate issuers, validity windows, renewal automation, and hybrid/PQC certificate roadmap.
findings.rule_name: RSA in TLS or certificate configuration

findings.reason: JWT signing algorithms protect authentication and authorization tokens and are high-priority migration inventory.
findings.recommendation: Identify issuers, verifiers, key rotation flows, and standards-compatible PQC or hybrid token signing options.

findings.reason: Private RSA keys are directly impacted by cryptographically relevant quantum computers and are high-value migration inventory.
findings.recommendation: Locate the owning service, rotate exposed sample material if needed, and plan migration to approved PQC or hybrid key establishment/signature approaches.
